---
title: "LRP72: Phonics route — letter-sound (L) × phoneme blending (B) -> decoding (nonword)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
```

## Overview

LRP72 is the **phonics-route** model, the second in the interaction family. It
tests the dual-route / triangle-model hypothesis that the two phonological
prerequisites — **letter-sound** knowledge $L$ (`yarclet`) and **phoneme
blending** $B$ (`blending`) — combine to bring word **decoding** online, measured
by `nonword` ($N$, the six monster nonwords). The hypothesised form is
**super-additive**: you need *both*, and each is worth more when the other is
present. This is the phonological sub-mechanism feeding the established
letter-sound → word-reading effect (LRP58).

The linear predictor (mechanism $= L$, moderator $= B$):

$$
\eta = \dots + \beta_{\text{mech}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L) + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\,z(B)
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L)\,z(B)
$$

$\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ would mean letter-sound converts to decoding *more*
strongly for higher-blending children (super-additive); $\gamma_{\text{int}} < 0$
is sub-additive (diminishing returns / partial substitutability).

::: {.callout-note title="Linear mechanism (GP off)"}

Decoding is a low, 57%-floored 0–6 count, so a nonparametric HSGP dose-response on
$\operatorname{logit} L$ is not identifiable. The mechanism enters as a single
linear slope $\beta_{\text{mech}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L)$
(`linear_mechanism=True`), so there is no nonparametric `f_mech` curve; the estimands
are the three coefficients above. In its place the *Mechanism curve* section below
plots the **linear** main-effect band $\beta_{\text{mech}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L)$
— the letter-sound dose-response at average blending.

:::

## Adjustment set — and why $W$ is excluded

Minimal set: $\{G, A, N_{\text{pre}}\}$ — group, age, and baseline decoding
(autoregressive).

::: {.callout-important title="DAG decision: word reading W is NOT conditioned on"}

Word reading $W$ is a sibling/descendant of decoding in the literacy cascade.
Conditioning on it would be **over-control / a collider risk** — the same error
class as the celf/LRP70 issue — and would bias the phonics → decoding estimand.
It is therefore excluded. Vocabulary and concepts are not plausible confounders of
the phonics → decoding path and are likewise left out. $L$ and $B$ are treated as
**parallel prerequisites**; the interaction estimand does not require resolving
whether $L$ precedes $B$.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Data caveat: floored outcome"}

`nonword` is 57% floored (median 0) but informative and developmentally moving
(28% → 60% of children decode ≥1 nonword across t1–t4). The predictors are
well-distributed (`yarclet`, `blending` each ~2% floored). The floor reduces
power and — importantly — means a **negative logit-scale interaction between two
strong positive main effects can partly reflect link saturation** (approaching the
count ceiling when both predictors are high), not necessarily substantive
antagonism. Interpret $\gamma_{\text{int}}$'s sign with that in mind.

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.png){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.png){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.png){width=40%}

`beta_mech` (the linear mechanism slope) uses a weakly-informative
$\mathrm{Normal}(0, 1)$ on the standardised $z(\operatorname{logit} L)$;
`gamma_mod` and `gamma_int` share the `gamma_cross` prior
$\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$.

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}
![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Coefficients

```{python}
# | echo: false
interaction = pd.read_csv(_here / "interaction_summary.csv")
interaction.T
```

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

**Reading the result.** Both prerequisites have **strong, credible positive main
effects** on decoding — letter-sound ($\beta_{\text{mech}}$) and blending
($\gamma_{\text{mod}}$, $P(>0) \approx 0.99$). Their **interaction
$\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is credibly *negative*** (95% CI just excludes zero,
$P(>0) \approx 0.02$) — i.e. *sub-additive*, the **opposite** of the hypothesised
super-additivity.

This **does not confirm** the dual-route super-additive prediction. Two important
qualifications:

- It **contradicts the exploratory pooled regression** (which found a positive
  $L \times B$ interaction, $t \approx 4.1$). That screen pooled ~4 rows/child
  without subject random effects or a baseline; the note flagged it as inflated by
  pseudo-replication. Under the proper model (subject random intercepts, baseline
  $N_{\text{pre}}$, phase intercepts) the sign **reverses** — exactly the
  confirmation step the exploration called for, and it fails.
- A negative *logit-scale* interaction between two strong positive main effects is
  partly what a **saturating link** produces on a floored/bounded count (see the
  data caveat). So the honest reading is: the data are **consistent with the two
  routes contributing largely independently** (additively), with at most modest,
  link-ambiguous evidence of diminishing returns — not with super-additive
  synergy.

## Mechanism curve

The linear dose-response of letter-sound knowledge on decoding: the posterior band
$\beta_{\text{mech}}\,z(\operatorname{logit} L)$, holding the adjustment set fixed and
evaluated at **average blending** (the interaction $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ tilts this
slope at other blending levels). A straight line by construction — the band is the
posterior uncertainty in the slope, not curvature.

![Mechanism curve](mechanism_curve.png){.lightbox}

```{python}
# | echo: false
mech = pd.read_csv(_here / "mechanism_curve.csv")
mech.head(15)
```

## PSIS-LOO vs the no-interaction baseline

LRP72base (`docs/models/lrp72base`) fits the same outcome / linear mechanism with
$L$ and $B$ as main effects only (no $L \times B$). Comparing them isolates the
interaction's predictive value
(`output/statistical_models/comparison/phonics_route_loo_compare.csv`):

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

The interaction model ranks first but the ELPD advantage is **within noise**
(elpd_diff ≈ 2 with dse ≈ 2.5) — adding the interaction does not decisively
improve predictive fit, consistent with the modest, link-ambiguous
$\gamma_{\text{int}}$.

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
